About Me

I'm Emma Hrinevitzky, a third-year B.Sc. Computer Science student at Ramat Gan Academic College — bringing a rare combination of academic performance and deep real-world infrastructure experience to everything I build.

Before collage, I spent over 7 years in technical support and network operations - from managing network infrastructure in the IDF to monitoring large-scale cloud systems as a NOC Engineer at Skai. I've resolved 99.2% of hardware and software incidents across thousands of users and know what it means to own a problem end-to-end in a production environment.

I'm currently seeking a student position in DevOps, infrastructure engineering, or software development somewhere I can apply my hands-on background, grow my automation and coding skills, and contribute to impactful projects.

02

Projects

2024 · Personal Project

PDF RAG Agent

A full-stack AI agent that lets users upload PDF documents and ask questions in natural language. Extracts and chunks text from PDFs, generates vector embeddings via Ollama, stores them in ChromaDB for semantic search, and uses a local LLaMA 3 model to produce cited answers. All services are orchestrated with Docker Compose.

Python Flask LangChain Ollama ChromaDB MongoDB Docker
2024 · UX/UI Course

UX/UI Course Presentation Site

Built a full presentation website for a UX/UI course assignment — solo. The site explains UX and UI principles through structured, visual storytelling. As part of the project, I also created a deliberate "bad UX" demo: a mock supermarket website designed to showcase common usability failures like confusing navigation, poor contrast, and broken flows — to illustrate the real impact of UX decisions.

HTML/CSS JavaScript Figma UX Research
View Live Site →
2024 · C++ Course

Dice Betting Game

A console-based betting game written in C++. Each round, the player bets an amount and guesses whether the sum of two dice will be even or odd — winning or losing their bet accordingly. The game ends when the player runs out of money or completes all rounds, then prints a full round-by-round summary. Uses deterministic seeding derived from student IDs for reproducibility.

C++ OOP Game Logic
2026

Chess Engine from Scratch

A fully playable chess engine built in C++ - implementing move generation, board evaluation, and a minimax algorithm with alpha-beta pruning. A deep dive into algorithms, game trees, and performance optimisation.

C++ Algorithms AI / Minimax
Coming Soon

Mood-Based Playlist Generator

An app that analyses the mood of a journal entry or a short text using NLP, then generates a Spotify playlist that matches the emotional tone. Type how you're feeling — get the perfect soundtrack.

Python Spotify API NLP React
